channel.title: The Grant Desk - Tech & Infrastructure, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Tech & Infrastructure, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Council of Europe: New Democratic Pact for Europe Democracy Hackathon 2026 (Strasbourg)
item.description:
Organisation: Council of Europe (under the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers), in partnership with No Hate Speech Week and Microsoft

Location: Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, France (hybrid: four online warm-up sessions leading to a three-day in-person finale)

Award: EUR 50,000 Microsoft-funded prize pool distributed among winning teams. Travel and accommodation covered for the 20 selected finalist teams. No application fee.

Duration: Online warm-up sessions plus three-day in-person hackathon (17-19 June 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

Council of Europe civic-tech hackathon to combat online hate speech and develop tech solutions for democracy, run alongside No Hate Speech Week and backed by a Microsoft prize pool. Four challenge tracks cover privacy-preserving hate speech detection, identifying coordinated inauthentic behaviour, gender-based violence reporting systems, and innovative social transformation approaches. ELIGIBILITY: pre-formed multidisciplinary teams of 3-4 (typically a technologist/developer, a democracy or policy expert, and a designer or UX innovator); residents of the 46 Council of Europe member states; coding experience is not required for every team member. FORMAT: four online warm-up sessions, then a three-day in-person finale at the Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, 17-19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wikimedia Conference Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Wikimedia Foundation

Location: Worldwide (subject to legal eligibility by country)

Award: From USD 10,000 per grant (no preset upper cap stated). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based. Processing approximately 3 months from submission to decision, plus 2-3 weeks for finalisation before disbursement.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Wikimedia Foundation grants supporting the organisation of local, regional and thematic conferences that bring Wikimedians together for knowledge sharing, skill development and professional networking. Two rounds per year. Round 1 deadline: 31 August 2026. Round 2 deadline: 1 February 2027. ELIGIBILITY: groups, organisations and Wikimedia affiliates; individuals may apply via a fiscal sponsor.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wikimedia Community Fund: General Support Fund (2026-27 cycles)
item.description:
Organisation: Wikimedia Foundation

Location: Worldwide; reviewed through regional committees (e.g., North America, CEE, MENA, Sub-Saharan Africa, etc.)

Award: Minimum USD 10,000 per year; regional maximums vary (e.g., up to USD 500,000/year in North America). The 2024-25 average for first-time grantees was approximately USD 33,000 globally. No application fee.

Duration: Minimum 6 months of programming; typically 12 months, with multi-year options available

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Wikimedia Foundation's largest community-side grant: multi-month or annual programming budgets supporting education, cultural heritage, gender and diversity, community engagement, free-knowledge advocacy and other Wikimedia-aligned work. ELIGIBILITY: individuals, groups, Wikimedia affiliates and organisations from the Wikimedia movement; grantees who currently hold a General Support Fund cannot simultaneously hold a Rapid Fund. CYCLES: two rounds per year, processed by regional review committees. Round 2 (2025-26) deadlines passed in early March 2026. Round 1 (2026-27) deadlines typically fall in late August / early September 2026, with exact dates varying by region - confirm the regional deadline on the linked page before applying. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wikimedia Hub Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Wikimedia Foundation

Location: Worldwide (groups working on Wikimedia hub initiatives)

Award: No preset minimum or maximum. Historical reference: research initiatives around USD 25,000; pilot-year hubs typically USD 50,000-110,000. Total pool USD 700,000 for the July 2025 - June 2026 fiscal cycle. No application fee.

Duration: Varies by stage: pre-pilot, pilot (approximately 1 year), or operations phases

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Wikimedia Foundation grants supporting hub initiatives - regional or thematic structures that coordinate Wikimedia work across multiple affiliates or contributors. Funds may cover staffing, programming, community support and operational expenses for hubs at pre-pilot, pilot or operational stages. ELIGIBILITY: groups only (not individuals). Applicants must be Wikimedia Movement groups composed of Wikimedia affiliates, unaffiliated organisations, organisers and/or project contributors working on a hub initiative. CYCLES: four quarterly rounds per annual pool (Round 1: 1 August; Round 2: 1 November; Round 3: 1 February; Round 4: 1 April). The 2025-26 cycle's final round closed 1 April 2026; the next annual pool (2026-27) is expected to follow the same quarterly pattern, with Round 1 likely 1 August 2026 - confirm on the linked page. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mozilla Festival (MozFest) 2026: Call for Proposals - Wilding
item.description:
Organisation: Mozilla Foundation

Location: Barcelona, Spain (Recinte Fabra i Coats; co-hosted with the City of Barcelona as part of Open Tech Week)

Award: Limited travel support, not guaranteed: flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform; a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla; a 5-day Metro pass including airport transfer. No additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance. Visa fee reimbursements handled through a separate process. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Three days, 28-30 October 2026

Deadline: Rolling / undated

MozFest 2026 is Mozilla Foundation's annual festival of debates, labs and workshops, forums, showcases, talks and installations - this year at Recinte Fabra i Coats in Barcelona (28-30 October 2026), co-hosted with the City of Barcelona during Open Tech Week. 2026 THEME 'WILDING': a provocation about loosening rigid systems and creating conditions for communities and technologies to evolve collectively, naturally and intuitively. Inspired by ecological rewilding, it explores community-owned mesh networks, federated platforms, open protocols, community archives, and tools built on transparency, trust and mutual flourishing. Mozilla is inviting proposals that exemplify Wilding and celebrate community-led alternatives - work that is proof of what is possible. FESTIVAL TRACKS: Wilding Advocacy; Wilding Knowledge; Wilding Systems; Wilding With Nature; Youth Wilding; Wilding Creativity; Founders Wilding; Developers Wilding. TRAVEL SUPPORT (limited; the festival cannot guarantee support for every applicant): flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform, a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla, and a 5-day Metro pass including the airport. Mozilla cannot provide additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance; visa fee reimbursements are handled through a separate process. There is no artist fee, stipend or honorarium. PROCESS: submit a proposal via the MozFest CFP on the Mozilla Foundation festival site; travel support requests are reviewed after the CFP closes. The CFP closing date is not stated in the linked page - verify the deadline on the MozFest site before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Robin Hood (via Blue Ridge Labs venture studio)

Location: United States (Phase 1 virtual; Phase 2 in-person at BRL Lab in Brooklyn, NYC)

Award: $20,000 stipend in Phase 2 to support MVP development. No equity terms mentioned. Includes weekly mentorship and coaching, workshops on product, growth and fundraising, co-working space at BRL Lab Brooklyn, access to the Design Insights Group (DIG) community for user testing, and network introductions across tech, philanthropy and policy.

Duration: Phase 1 (Explore & Test): 6 to 8 weeks, June to July 2026, virtual-first. Phase 2 (Build MVP): 10 to 12 weeks, September to November 2026, in-person Brooklyn.

Deadline: Closed 03 May 2026

Founder fellowship for early-stage or new founders tackling problems in Robin Hood's five economic-mobility focus areas: Education, Financial Empowerment, Health, Housing, and Hunger & Jobs. Applicants need lived or learned experience with the challenges they propose to address. No prototype or co-founder required at application. Approximately 25 founders advance from Phase 1 to Phase 2. Open to US applicants only; no relocation expected for Phase 1, but Phase 2 requires in-person time at the BRL Lab in Brooklyn. Apply via the Airtable application form linked from the Robin Hood page; submit an idea summary and background. Fellowship is fully funded by Robin Hood (no application fee, no equity stake disclosed). Note: this is a startup/entrepreneur-style fellowship rather than an arts/research fellowship; applicants should be building venture-track ideas tied to economic mobility outcomes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pace Research Fellowship Summer 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Pace

Location: Pace HQ, New York City, US

Award: $10,000 stipend + access to compute and the Pace investment-team network

Duration: 4 weeks (July 2026)

Deadline: Closed 16 May 2026

Four-week paid summer residency for technologists working at the intersection of computing infrastructure, economics and the physical world. Fellows work alongside the Pace investment team and leave with original, publishable research artifacts (one sharp public piece on the systems shaping the next decade). Open to students, researchers, founders and independent thinkers who want real room to go deep on infrastructure and economics questions. Hosted at Pace HQ in NYC.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wikimedia Foundation Rapid Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Wikimedia Foundation

Location: Worldwide (subject to legal eligibility by country)

Award: $500 to $5,000 USD per grant (distributed in local-currency equivalent at time of application). Per-applicant cap $10,000/fiscal year; individuals may not hold multiple open Rapid Funds, organisations/groups limited to 2 open grants at a time.

Duration: 1 to 12 months per project

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

Quick-turnaround grants for individuals, Wikimedia community members, groups and affiliates running short-term, low-cost Wikimedia-focused projects: editathons, workshops, community meetups, education projects, cultural heritage initiatives, gender/diversity programmes, small-scale software development and content campaigns. Five cycles per year; upcoming deadlines: 1 July 2026, 1 September 2026, 1 November 2026, 1 February 2027, 1 April 2027. Approximately 2 months processing time per cycle. Standard track and Technical Projects track (Grants:Project/Rapid/Tech) share deadlines and amounts. Apply via the Wikimedia Foundation Grantee Portal (Fluxx) at https://wmf.fluxx.io/. Transfers run slower in June and December. CEE region applications routed via the CEE Hub from 1 April 2026. Ineligible: General Support Fund grantees, applicants on SDN lists, those with recent UCoC violations.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI Fediversity (11th call)
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI Fediversity (funded by EU Horizon Europe)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants for projects making federated and self-hosted internet services easier to deploy and operate at scale. Funds practical work on portable Fediverse-style infrastructure, hosted Mastodon-like or PeerTube-like services, secure email, private cloud and other tools that support decentralisation and digital self-determination. Open to individuals and organisations of any type; results released under free or open-source licences.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fab City Awards 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Fab City

Location: Anywhere in the world

Award: €1,000 per category winner (3) + €1,500 Overall Impact Award + up to USD 600 in Seeed Studio products + mentoring + free FAB26 Boston ticket

Duration: Winners announced 3 June 2026; showcase at FAB26 Boston 27 to 31 July 2026

Deadline: Closed 29 Apr 2026

The Fab City Awards recognise implemented and piloted initiatives showing how collective action can shape real transitions in cities and regions. 2026 theme: Collective Action for Regenerative Urban Futures. Looking for initiatives that combine design, technology and civic engagement; connect scientific knowledge with local know-how; demonstrate real on-the-ground implementation; and show potential to scale across cities and regions. Projects, platforms, policies, infrastructures or community-led programmes that are already piloted/implemented with evidence and documentation. Three categories: Community (participation, organisation, local empowerment), Technology (accessible distributed tech with social value), Nature (conservation, restoration, ecosystem protection). Three category winners each receive €1,000 seed funding plus up to USD 600 in Seeed Studio products, optional technical guidance, potential lab access (plus Fab Lab Puebla and Litchee Lab for initiatives based there), mentoring with the Fab City ecosystem, a feature across Fab City channels, and a free ticket to present at FAB26 in Boston (July 2026). One Overall Impact Award (€1,500 + the same network/mentoring/FAB26 benefits) goes to the project with the strongest systemic integration across all dimensions. One Thomas Duggan Award is given as a special distinction for Arts and Design. Open to individuals and teams across public, private and civic sectors, including for-profit and nonprofit organisations, collectives, public institutions, labs and makerspaces, community groups, NGOs, academia and companies, anywhere in the world. Evaluation 30 April to 26 May 2026; winners announced 3 June 2026. Powered by Fab City; supported by Seeed Studio, Fab Foundation, Ibero Puebla, and Litchee Lab.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: OTF Internet Freedom Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Open Technology Fund (OTF)

Location: Worldwide

Award: $10,000 to $900,000 (sweet spot $50,000 to $200,000)

Duration: Up to 24 months (ideal: 6-12 months)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Rolling-deadline fund for technology-focused projects that promote human rights, internet freedom and open societies. Funds anti-censorship, anti-surveillance, privacy-preserving and circumvention tools, plus applied research. Two-stage process: submit a Concept Note via the OTF online application system; reviewed monthly with feedback in 6-8 weeks. Open internationally.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Internet Society Foundation: Research Grant Program 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Internet Society Foundation

Location: Worldwide

Award: $200,000 to $500,000 per project

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 22 May 2026

Research grants for innovative work deepening understanding of the Internet and its impact on society. Four focus areas: Inclusive Internet (structural barriers to digital participation), Greening the Internet (environmental impact and sustainability), Measuring Meaningful Connectivity (frameworks including AI, cloud, platform ecosystems), and A Trustworthy Internet (reliability, security, accountability). Open to individual researchers and organisations worldwide; underrepresented groups and Global Majority applicants strongly encouraged. Applications via Fluxx in English, French or Spanish.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI Fediversity (11th call): Hosting Stack of the Future
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI Fediversity (funded by EU Horizon Europe)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (€450,000 total budget for open calls)

Duration: Project-based (programme runs to November 2027)

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants for projects that strengthen Fediversity, an effort to bring easy-to-use hosted cloud services with service portability and personal freedom at their core to everyone. Built on NixOS for declarative, reproducible, reliable deployment of Fediverse services (PeerTube, Mastodon, Owncast, Lemmy), email, VPN, private cloud storage, wikis and more. Project results released under open source licences.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI TALER (13th call): Privacy-Preserving Digital Payments
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI TALER (with GNU Taler community, funded by EU Horizon Europe and Swiss SERI)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (€676,000 total budget for open calls)

Duration: Project-based (programme runs to November 2026)

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants for free and open source efforts aligned with privacy-preserving digital payments. Build new GNU Taler capabilities, auxiliary tools, UX, FOSS-app and open-standard integrations (P2P micropayments in messengers, social media, video conferencing), or merchant-backend improvements. All results released under a free or open-source licence. Strong projects can scale up via NGI0 Core.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI Zero Commons Fund (13th call)
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI Zero (funded by EU Horizon Europe and Swiss SERI)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (scalable for proven potential)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants to help reclaim the public nature of the internet across the full technology spectrum: libre silicon, middleware, P2P infrastructure, end-user applications, open standards, open data and AI, open science, creative commons and open educational resources. Project results released under free or open-source licences. Aimed at troubleshooters tackling hard, important internet-commons challenges. Total programme: €21.6m through 2027.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
